name: art19.com
prose:
# ART19 Content API Documentation API

The ART19 Content API conforms to the [JSON:API specification](http://jsonapi.org). API requests **MUST** use the HTTP Accept header: `Accept: application/vnd.api+json` API requests **MUST** be authenticated using the HTTP Authorization header: `Authorization: Token token="your-token", credential="your-credential"` ## General Notes Some query parameters use unencoded [ and ] characters simply for 

## Getting started

The ART19 Content API Documentation API is a JSON REST API. All endpoints are relative to the base URL `https://art19.com`.

## Authentication

Authenticate by passing your API key in the `Authorization` header on every request.

## API reference

### GET /classification_inclusions
Get ClassificationInclusion records
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `ids[]`, `page[number]`, `page[size]`, `classification_id`, `classification_type`, `classified_id`, `classified_type`, `q`, `sort`.

Sample response:

```json
{ "data": [ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ] }
```

### GET /classification_inclusions/{id}
Get a specific classification inclusion
Returns a single record.

### GET /classifications
Get a list of classifications
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `ids[]`, `page[number]`, `page[size]`, `type`, `q`, `is_country`, `sort`.

Sample response:

```json
{ "data": [ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ] }
```

### GET /classifications/{id}
Get a specific classification
Returns a single record.

### GET /credits
Get a list of credits
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `ids[]`, `page[number]`, `page[size]`, `creditable_id`, `creditable_type`, `sort`.

Sample response:

```json
{ "data": [ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ] }
```

### GET /credits/{id}
Get a specific credit
Returns a single record.

### GET /episodes
Get a list of episodes
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `ids[]`, `page[number]`, `page[size]`, `included_in_inventory_calendar`, `month`, `published`, `q`, `released_after`, `released_before`, `rss`, `season_id`, `series_id`.

Sample response:

```json
{ "data": [ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ] }
```

### GET /episodes/{id}
Get a specific episode
Returns a single record.

### GET /episodes/{id}/next_sibling
Get the episode released right after the specified one
Returns a single record.

### GET /episodes/{id}/previous_sibling
Get the episode released right before the specified one
Returns a single record.

{ "data": [ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ] }

Returns a single record.
